item.title: Cancer drug licensed by GSK from China posts promising trial results
item.description: Mo-Rez, developed by Hansoh Pharma, is a next-generation treatment for ovarian and endometrial cancer

item.title: How to deal with NHS doctors in revolt
item.description: The Conservative plan to ban strikes is doomed to fail

item.title: UK financial regulators rush to assess risks of Anthropic’s latest AI model
item.description: Leading banks, insurers and exchanges to be warned over cyber security vulnerabilities exposed by Claude Mythos

item.title: Trump has not done enough to tackle soaring housing costs, real estate billionaire says
item.description: Republican donor Stephen Ross argues housing affordability is ‘going to be the biggest issue’ in US

item.title: Musk hits legal losing streak ahead of showdown with OpenAI’s Altman
item.description: The world’s richest man has lost in cases ranging from shareholder fraud to claims that his rivals stole AI secrets

item.title: How to make shoplifting a crime with consequences
item.description: Police, shop owners and policymakers must combine to fight organised retail theft

item.title: Leonard Green Partners to buy construction consultancy for $3bn
item.description: Acquisition comes amid slow patch for private equity dealmaking as interest rates stay high

item.title: Robinhood excludes some prediction markets over manipulation fears
item.description: US broker says it is ‘very focused on insider trading’ as it pushes into fast-growing sector

item.title: Commodity traders lost ‘billions’ in early days of Iran war
item.description: Firms that normally profit from volatility were caught out by sudden rise in energy prices, new report finds

item.title: Fees for seas: a history of taxing waterways
item.description: From Ottoman Sultans to Danish kings, Iran’s toll system for Strait of Hormuz revives an old maritime fight

item.title: Aston Martin shares and bonds sink to record lows over cash crunch fears
item.description: One credit investor says it is unclear who will be luxury-car maker’s latest ‘white knight’

item.title: Social media is for picking up vibes, not information
item.description: Users trust it even less than they do mainstream news outlets

item.title: Virgin Media O2 boss: before prostate cancer, ‘I thought I was a bit invincible’
item.description: Telecoms boss has juggled personal challenges with the competing priorities of two sides in a high-stakes merger

item.title: Switzerland’s Zug becomes bolt-hole for Gulf-based wealth
item.description: Small Swiss town is welcoming individuals and companies seeking refuge from war in the Middle East

item.title: Wall Street banks set to report $40bn trading haul as Iran war rekindles volatility
item.description: Five largest US lenders expected to unveil highest combined trading revenues since at least 2014

item.title: Tropical Chinese island bids to be world’s largest free-trade hub
item.description: Critics ask if biggest economic reforms for decades will pay off for remote province

item.title: Will Google’s TurboQuant algorithm hurt AI demand for memory chips?
item.description: More efficient artificial intelligence could mean even greater need for semiconductors, say experts

item.title: How will AI change the org chart?
item.description: At the micro level, there is evidence that it pays to collapse the layers of command

item.title: Richard Caring sells majority stake in Ivy hospitality empire to Sheikh Tahnoon’s IHC
item.description: Deal includes an investment of more than £1bn

item.title: NFL’s media rights stand-off hits a plot twist
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: what Sunderland says about women’s football, cricket’s vote of confidence in the Gulf as a sports hub, and more

item.title: Superyacht maker Sanlorenzo rides personalisation wave
item.description: Italian group looks to sustain sales boom with hyper bespoke vessels including its latest built around a living tree

item.title: The damage wrought on the Middle East’s oil and gas supplies
item.description: Saudi Arabia and Qatar have both suffered significant damage to production capacity during the US-Israeli war against Iran

item.title: Jacob’s Creek should come in smaller bottles
item.description: Relaunch of popular 1990s tipple might need more than nostalgia to stand out from today’s crowd

item.title: Mortgage borrowers seek shorter-term deals as market volatility saps confidence
item.description: Flexibility is the watchword among UK borrowers amid signs of slowing activity in the housing market

item.title: BP’s proxy fight exposes bugs in shareholder voting machine
item.description: On contentious matters, investors are less likely to follow proxies

item.title: UK households turn to solar panels as energy bills bite
item.description: Fallout from Middle East crisis prompts more people to explore the alternative source of power

item.title: The UK police’s latest problem: crowds of unruly teens
item.description: Disorder in London and other cities threatens public sense of order, even though it may fall short of widespread criminality

item.title: Tofoo co-founder: ‘I’d lost £250,000 in investments before I started the company’
item.description: It was fourth time lucky for entrepreneur David Knibbs

item.title: Separating the signal from the noise in private credit
item.description: What matters most is how capital was deployed and underwritten

item.title: Anthropic closes in on OpenAI as US business use surges
item.description: Divergence reflects company’s recent rapid growth owing to strong interest in its Claude Code products
